[2018-02-03 13:43] elyot: The whole pack-buying and deck building business model forces them to take a harmfully slow approach to balance fixing so that buyers have confidence in their purchase

[2018-02-04 05:41] weiseguy: I think the argument that every format has always been bad if solved just isn't true. For example, the Scars/Innistrad/M13 format was excellent, both in terms of diversity of archetypes (delver, pod, and zombies were the tier 1 decks, with delver being the "best" deck but having slightly unfavorable matchups vs the other tier 1 decks, and with fringe BW tokens and control decks also being fairly playable, if not quite as good) and in the gameplay being interesting & skill-testing. The decks were interesting to play, the builds were not 100% agreed upon for the different archetypes, and there were just many ways to get an edge. I can probably come up with more examples of great formats if you want, my point is that I don't think there's any underlying truth about how every format becomes poor if examined enough -- there are plenty of good formats and plenty of bad formats, and people are just upset right now because the last several have been bad for a variety of reasons.
[2018-02-04 05:49] mundungu: there is definitely something to be said for information sharing the past couple of years
[2018-02-04 05:50] mundungu: it is incredibly difficult to design / develop fun, fresh, balanced sets for mtg because of the pack pushing constraints and the inability to make any patches
[2018-02-04 05:50] mundungu: it just seems like an impossible task compared to the digital ccgs, especially with the dated business model
[2018-02-04 05:57] weiseguy: I think if the people designing the game were as good as the top players playing the game there wouldn't be an issue
[2018-02-04 05:57] weiseguy: hopefully the new Play Design thing helps in that regard
[2018-02-04 05:58] weiseguy: they've been running into issues because "oh wow, didn't realize this kitty combos with saheeli rai" when literally every decent player I know saw that interaction within 2 minutes of reading the card
[2018-02-04 06:01] weiseguy: another example: the errata they had to make to hostake taker because they didn't realize that it could very easily draw the game
[2018-02-04 06:03] weiseguy: or "wow, never thought that in this creature-centric & removal-light metagame we are pushing, this aggressively costed colorless smuggler's copter card that works well with creatures would be a 4 of in just about every deck"

Also imo if someone wants to look up the solutions to a puzzle and then later regrets it that's on them and not a strong reason against making walkthroughs.
